_comment: Macro-Terrain: Ancient God Rib Canyon. Per scale directive: this terrain IS a being. Player shouldn't initially realize they're standing on bone. Divine Sense -> Perception changes -> 'You realize this mountain range... is one finger.' Prime Directive: the being's corpse is objectively terrain; perception tier determines what the player sees.
name: Ancient God Rib Canyon
scale: 500 li long, 50 li wide, 10 li tall — the ribcage of a continent-spanning being.
canonical_event: Tu Si's ribcage formed this canyon when his god-body fell.

perception_tiered_appearance.mortal: A canyon 500 li long, walls of curved grey stone arching overhead like ribs.
perception_tiered_appearance.qi_condensation: The walls hum in storms; the canyon amplifies sound strangely.
perception_tiered_appearance.foundation_establishment: The walls are petrified ribs; the canyon is the space between them.
perception_tiered_appearance.core_formation: This is an Ancient God's ribcage; the canyon runs between two ribs.
perception_tiered_appearance.nascent_soul: The ribs still hold residual god-essence; marrow-channels line the walls.
perception_tiered_appearance.spirit_severing: The ribcage protected Tu Si's heart; cultivating here grants body-tempering essence.

perception_mechanic.trigger: When player's perception tier rises, the appearance re-rolls automatically.
perception_mechanic.no_locked_upgrades: A mortal who later reaches Core Formation will see the truth; the terrain does not change, only understanding does.
perception_mechanic.canon_fidelity: This is exactly how Wang Lin perceived Ancient God Tu Si's remains — first as terrain, then as god-bone, then as god-memory.
